title: ranunculus cassubicus subsp. schwarzii
intro: Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. schwarzii, also known as the Schwarz's meadow buttercup, is a plant species in the buttercup family that is native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. schwarzii include its potential to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in topical creams, ointments, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
